evaluation.summary: Measured variance-to-mean ratios for primorial gaps scale as R(k) proportional to (log P_k)^{0.80}, showing lower values than expected and contradicting the previously suggested exponent of 1.17.
next_directions: Extend the analysis to larger k (e.g., up to k=15 or higher) to assess scaling stability.
Develop a rigorous theoretical framework to explain the observed exponent and its deviation from prior models.
Systematically study boundary and truncation effects across different primorial ranges to validate the current mitigation strategies.
